{{Infobox artifact
| name = Subspace Drive
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Subspace Drive Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A [[Subspace]] [[Drive]] is a specialized propulsion system used to facilitate long-distance transportation by allowing vessels and certain individuals to enter [[Subspace]]. This process, often referred to as a dive, involves creating a warp or tear in reality that enables travel through a dimension characterized by darkness and complex patterns. While [[Subspace]] itself is vast, travel is limited by practical factors such as fuel, stability, and the amount of matter pulled into the dimension during a dive; dragging too much matter can cause violent storms or physical damage to a ship. These drives vary significantly in scale, ranging from massive cores housed in large docking rooms to tiny internal versions integrated into an individual's body. While standard starship drives allow for significant travel, smaller or less efficient versions are limited to micro-dives that only skim the surface of [[Subspace]]. The technology can be enhanced by specialized pilots or powered by magic, such as an [[Aether Drive]]. However, the technology carries risks, including the potential for immediate drive ruptures, dimensional breaches, or being targeted by [[Subspace]] creatures that seek the drives as a food source. Additionally, travelers may face "[[Subspace madness]]," a condition where individuals become obsessed with the visual patterns of the dimension.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Subspace]] [[Drive]] is a starship component consisting of a large, twisting, and semi-transparent tube that allows a vessel to enter [[Subspace]]. While routing power to the device can cause an instant rupture and [[binding]]-spells are capable of striking it, the drive can also be delayed from a distance. Although the narrator's ship lacks this technology, the Last [[Horizon]] utilizes one. Without a [[Pilot]], [[Horizon]] is limited to her default drive, but the addition of a [[Pilot]] allows the device to evolve and regain its legendary heights. Through this evolution, the [[Subspace]] [[Drive]] can be restored to a state of peace and order.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Subspace Drive]] is a device used to enter [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c7">''The Captain'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Routing power to the [[Subspace Drive]] causes an instant [[Drive rupture]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator's ship lacks a [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Pilot]]'s gifts evolve the [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Subspace Drive]] can be delayed from a distance.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Subspace Drive]] is a component of starships.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19">''The Captain'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Binding-spells can strike a [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c27">''The Captain'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Without a [[Pilot]], [[Horizon]] is stuck with her default [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Subspace Drive]]'s core is a large, twisting, semi-transparent tube.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38">''The Captain'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Subspace Drive]] can be restored to peace and order.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c38" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Horizon]]'s [[Subspace Drive]] has regained its legendary heights with the addition of a [[Pilot]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41">''The Captain'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]] utilizes a [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|As a key component of the envisioned fleet, [[Subspace Drives]] are utilized by vessels such as the [[Starhammer]], though its internal version is less efficient than that of a true starship. On [[The Last Horizon]], the drive operates incorrectly and faces a mere fourteen percent chance of reaching [[Subspace]] without rupturing. This specific unit requires repairs, especially given that the drive can trigger warning alarms during operation. Furthermore, using a drive to dive into [[Subspace]] within a planetary atmosphere is difficult for large ships and causes surrounding matter to be pulled along with the vessel.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Subspace Drive]] on [[The Last Horizon]] has a fourteen percent chance of reaching [[Subspace]] without rupture.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c2">''The Engineer'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] has an internal [[Subspace Drive]] that is less efficient than a real starship's.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Diving into [[Subspace]] within a planet's atmosphere is difficult for large ships.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15">''The Engineer'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Diving into [[Subspace]] pulls surrounding matter with the ship.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The ship utilizes a [[Subspace Drive]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c16">''The Engineer'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Subspace Drive]] of [[The Last Horizon]] is not working correctly.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Subspace Drives]] are a component of the envisioned fleet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Horizon]]'s [[Subspace Drive]] needs to be repaired.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Subspace Drive]] can trigger warning alarms.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Subspace Drive]] is something the narrator prepares.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Subspace Drive]] supports micro-dives.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator triggered their [[Subspace Drive]] to emerge.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A [[Subspace]] [[Drive]] is a mechanism utilized to facilitate travel through subspace. During the events involving the [[Vallenar]] ship, the drive was employed to undock and begin a departure. Within the [[Karoshan]] facility, isolated cores of these drives exist with the potential to rupture should a dimensional breach occur. The crew also relied on a [[Subspace]] [[Drive]] to execute a retreat. However, it was noted that the drive would fail if [[Omega]] tried to travel an excessive distance through the [[Subspace lock]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A [[Subspace Drive]] is used for subspace travel.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c1">''The Pilot'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Vallenar]] ship uses a [[Subspace Drive]] to undock and depart.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c12">''The Pilot'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Karoshan]] facility contains isolated [[Subspace Drive]] cores that can rupture in case of a dimensional breach.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The crew used a [[Subspace Drive]] to retreat.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Subspace Drive]] would fail if [[Omega]] attempted to travel too far through the [[Subspace lock]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47">''The Pilot'', chapter 47</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|"Says here that we have about a fourteen percent chance of reaching Subspace without an immediate Drive rupture."<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c2" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Artifacts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon artifacts]]
